sound_type: no audible vocalization + rooster-tail splash -- echolocation >100 kHz (above human hearing). Physical sound: explosive V-shaped spray at 35 mph. E=5
ecosystem: Open water, deep channels, continental shelf. Resident in Puget Sound and Strait of Juan de Fuca year-round.
season: Year-round resident. More commonly sighted fall/winter. Does not migrate from region.

coordinates.description: Dall's Porpoise in Strait of Juan de Fuca / San Juan Islands. Deep channel habitat. Bow-riding boats.

taxonomy_note.description: FIRST PHOCOENIDAE -- porpoise family enters. First cetacean in the SPS engine. Narrow-Band High-Frequency (NBHF) echolocation as family trait. Porpoise vs dolphin distinction.
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Phocoenoides from Greek 'phokaena' (porpoise) + '-oides' (resembling). dalli after W.H. Dall (naturalist). 'Dall's Porpoise' for its discoverer.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Phocoenoides -- monotypic genus. Only species: P. dalli.
taxonomy_note.order_note: Cetacea. FIRST Cetacea in SPS. Whales, dolphins, porpoises. New order alongside Carnivora.
taxonomy_note.family_position: Fourth marine mammal family: Phocidae (180-181), Otariidae (182-184), Mustelidae (185), Phocoenidae (186). First cetacean family.
taxonomy_note.class_note: Class Mammalia. Marine mammal era: 7 consecutive (180-186). Now 4 families.

song_metrics.song_duration_s: Click trains: continuous during echolocation. Rooster-tail: continuous at speed.
song_metrics.note_structure: NBHF clicks (ultrasonic, inaudible). Rooster-tail splash (physical, audible).
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Click trains during navigation/hunting. Rooster tail during high-speed travel.
song_metrics.timbre: Echolocation: ultrasonic clicks (inaudible). Splash: explosive, percussive.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=5. Silent voice + explosive physical presence. Energy from kinetic splash, not vocal.
song_metrics.tempo: Click trains: rapid, continuous. Splash: sustained at speed.
song_metrics.context: Deep channel waters. Surface during travel. Bow-riding boats.
song_metrics.regional_variation: Minimal documented variation in NBHF clicks.
song_metrics.key_property: NO AUDIBLE VOCALIZATION + ROOSTER-TAIL SPLASH. FIRST PHOCOENIDAE. NBHF stealth echolocation. 35 mph. E=5. v1.49.327.

pelage.overall: Black body with large white flank/belly patch. Dorsal fin often white-tipped. Thick, muscular body. Built for speed and power.
pelage.diagnostic_mark: Black-and-white tuxedo pattern. Rooster-tail splash at speed. Robust, thick body. Small triangular dorsal fin (often white-tipped).
pelage.swimming_pattern: High-speed surface swimming with rooster-tail spray. Bow-riding. Dives to 500+ meters.

physical_description.overall: Length: 1.8-2.2 m. Weight: 130-220 kg. Thick, muscular body. Small head. Small mouth with spade-shaped teeth. Robust for a porpoise.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Black-and-white pattern. Rooster-tail splash. Thick body. Small dorsal fin. Spade-shaped teeth (porpoise, not dolphin).
physical_description.field_id: Black-and-white small cetacean at high speed with distinctive rooster-tail spray. Thick body. Often bow-riding boats. Much smaller than orca. No visible beak.

nodes.title: First Phocoenidae -- Porpoises Enter
nodes.description: First porpoise in the engine. First cetacean. New order (Cetacea). Phocoenidae family. NBHF echolocation as family-level trait. Porpoise vs dolphin distinction.

nodes.title: Rooster Tail -- Speed as Sound
nodes.description: 35 mph. V-shaped spray. Visible from 1+ mile. The 'sound' of a silent animal is its kinetic presence. E=5 from explosive splash, not vocalization.

nodes.title: Stealth Echolocation -- NBHF Clicks
nodes.description: >100 kHz. Above human AND orca hearing. Navigate and hunt invisibly. Anti-predator adaptation. Silence as survival strategy. Phocoenidae family trait.

cross_references.relationship: PHOCOENIDAE PAIR! Dall's (186, E=5) + Harbor (187, E=1). Same family. Speed vs stealth. Rooster tail vs pure silence.
MARINE MAMMAL ERA! Mustelidae (185) to Phocoenidae (186). New family. 7 consecutive marine mammals.
CETACEAN PROGRESSION! Porpoise (186-187) to dolphin (188). Phocoenidae to Delphinidae. First to third cetacean.
THE CROSSOVER! Campbell crosses gospel/hip-hop. Dall's crosses deep water/explosive surface at 35 mph. Both defined by crossing energy.
